title: brassicaceae
intro: The Brassicaceae rank, also known as the mustard or cabbage family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes species such as broccoli, cauliflower, kale, cabbage, and mustard. This family is characterized by its unique four-petaled flowers, which often have a distinctive cross-like or cruciform shape. The Brassicaceae family is comprised of over 338 genera and approximately 4,000 species, making it one of the largest and most widespread plant families globally. Many species within this family are known for their edible leaves, stems, or seeds, which are prized for their culinary and nutritional value.
